Description: Blood disorder that obstructs production of hemaglobin in blood.
Functional effect: Start loss due to disruption of the initiator methionine

Description: Cystic fibrosis is an inherited disease characterized by the buildup of thick, sticky mucus that can damage many of the body's organs. The disorder's most common signs and symptoms include progressive damage to the respiratory system and chronic digestive system problems.
Functional effect: May impact translation initiation or efficiency

Description: Tay-Sachs disease is a rare, inherited disorder that is characterized by neurological problems caused by  the death of nerve cells (neurons) in the brain and spinal cord (central nervous system).
Functional effect: Nonsense mediated decay, truncation, or a dysfunctional protein product

Description: A carcinoma that arises from the lung and is characterized by the presence of malignant glandular epithelial cells. Usually lung adenocarcinoma is asymptomatic and is identified through screening studies or as an incidental radiologic finding. If clinical symptoms are present they include shortness of breath, cough, hemoptysis, chest pain, and fever. Tobacco smoke is a known risk factor.
